question: What are the key architectural changes introduced in the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform compared to Blackwell?
findings: The Vera Rubin platform is built from six new chips: NVIDIA Vera CPU (88 custom Olympus cores), NVIDIA Rubin GPU (with HBM4 and new Transformer Engine), NVIDIA NVLink 6 switch, NVIDIA ConnectX-9, NVIDIA BlueField-4 DPU, and NVIDIA Spectrum-6 Ethernet switch.

The Vera Rubin platform treats the data center—not a single GPU server—as the unit of compute, with GPUs, CPUs, networking, security, software, power delivery, and cooling architected together as a single system.
Each Vera Rubin superchip integrates two Rubin GPUs with one Vera CPU via memory-coherent NVLink-C2C, collapsing traditional CPU-GPU boundaries into a unified rack-scale execution domain.

The Rubin GPU incorporates HBM4 memory, doubling interface width vs. HBM3e, with up to 288 GB per GPU and aggregate bandwidth of up to 22 TB/s—nearly tripling memory bandwidth compared to Blackwell (~8 TB/s).
NVLink 6 doubles GPU-to-GPU scale-up bandwidth to 3.6 TB/s per GPU (from 1.8 TB/s in Blackwell/NVLink 5), and NVLink-C2C doubles to 1.8 TB/s (from 900 GB/s), while PCIe remains at 256 GB/s (Gen 6).
